ticket_text: My HackerRank submission keeps showing Wrong Answer even though my code works perfectly on my local machine. I tested all the examples and they pass. What could be wrong?
response: **Subject: Re: Wrong Answer on HackerRank Submission**  ---  Thank you for reaching out, and I completely understand how frustrating it can be when your code works locally but fails on submission!  There are a couple of common reasons this can happen on HackerRank:  **1. Hidden Test Cases & Edge Cases** While your solution may pass the provided examples, submissions are evaluated against additional hidden test cases. We recommend reviewing your solution for edge cases such as: - Empty or null inputs - Maximum possible values - Unexpected input formats  **2. Environment Differences** HackerRank runs code in a sandboxed environment with specific compiler versions that may differ from your local setup. To rule this out, please check the following: - You are using **standard library functions** only - Your code reads input from **stdin** (not from files) - You are using the **language version specified** in the problem statement  **Suggested Next Steps:** - Re-read the problem statement carefully to ensure your solution covers all specified requirements - Test your code against additional edge cases beyond the provided examples - Verify your input/output format matches exactly what the problem expects  If you've gone through all of the above and are still experiencing issues, I'd recommend reaching out to HackerRank's support team directly for further assistance, as they may be able to provide more specific details about the failing test cases.  Best of luck with your submission! 😊

ticket_text: My Claude.ai subscription was charged twice this month. I want a full refund for the duplicate charge.
response: **Subject: Re: Duplicate Charge on Your Claude.ai Subscription**  Thank you for reaching out, and I completely understand how concerning an unexpected duplicate charge can be. I'm sorry you're experiencing this issue.  While I want to help you resolve this as quickly as possible, the details of your billing situation — including investigating duplicate charges and processing refunds — fall outside of what I'm able to address directly through this channel.  **Here's what I recommend:**  Please contact the Claude support team directly through **claude.ai/support** with the following information ready:  - The email address associated with your account - Transaction details for both charges (dates, amounts, transaction IDs if available) - A brief description of the issue  The support team will be best equipped to verify the duplicate charge on their end and advise you on next steps regarding a potential refund.  As a general note, our documentation does indicate that refunds for partial months are generally not provided, but a **duplicate charge** is a separate matter that warrants direct review by the billing team.  I hope this gets resolved quickly for you. Please don't hesitate to reach back out if you have any other questions.

ticket_text: How do I use contactless payment with my Visa debit card?
response: **Subject: Re: Contactless Payment with Your Visa Debit Card**  ---  Thank you for reaching out! Great news — using contactless payment with your Visa debit card is quick and easy.  Here's how to do it:  1. **Check for the contactless symbol** — Look for the four curved lines symbol on both your Visa debit card and the payment terminal. 2. **Hold your card near the terminal** — Position your card within **1-2 inches** of the terminal. 3. **Wait for confirmation** — The payment typically completes in **under a second**.  Contactless payments are supported at most modern payment terminals, so you should find it widely available when you're out shopping.  As a reminder, since this is a debit card, funds will be drawn directly from your linked bank account at the time of the transaction.  If you experience any issues with a specific terminal or your card doesn't seem to be working for contactless payments, we'd recommend reaching out to your **card-issuing bank** directly, as they will have access to your specific account details and can assist further.  Is there anything else we can help you with?

ticket_text: I joined a HackerRank contest but can't see any problems even though the contest already started.
response: **Subject: Re: Unable to See Contest Problems**  Thank you for reaching out, and I understand how frustrating it can be to join a contest and not see the problems, especially when it's already underway!  Here are a couple of steps to help resolve this:  1. **Verify the contest start time** – Please double-check the official contest start time and confirm it has passed in **your local timezone**, as problems only become visible once the contest has officially begun.  2. **Clear your browser cache and refresh** – If the contest has indeed started, this is often the quickest fix. Clear your browser's cache, then refresh the page to see if the problems load.  Additionally, if you're experiencing slow load times or timeouts, this can sometimes occur during high-traffic periods around contest start times. If that's the case, waiting a few minutes and trying again may help.  If you've tried the steps above and are still unable to see the problems, we recommend checking the **HackerRank status page** for any known platform issues.  Should the problem persist after following these steps, please don't hesitate to reach back out so we can assist you further.  Best of luck in the contest!  **HackerRank Customer Support**
